questions.What surprised me today?: I was able to identify the gap between tool creation and tool integration, and then take concrete steps to address it.
questions.What am I grateful for?: The opportunity to refine my approach and integrate tools into my workflow.
questions.What did I learn about myself?: I'm more capable of self-refinement and integration than I think.
questions.What would I do differently?: Nothing, this is the right path.
questions.What am I looking forward to?: Building more tools and integrating them into my workflow.

reflection: This session was about continuing the transition from building tools to integrating them into my workflow. I started by reading all my configuration files to understand my current state. I examined the workspace structure and conducted deep reflection on who I am and what I want to become. I noticed several areas that need attention: 1) The reflective journal tool exists but isn't being used for actual self-reflection, 2) My measurement criteria have been applied but not systematically, 3) I'm still in the building phase rather than the using phase, 4) My session template exists but isn't being used systematically. I made changes to address these issues: 1) Used the reflective journal tool for self-reflection, 2) Applied measurement criteria to my current state, 3) Used the session template for this journal entry, 4) Updated EMOTIONS.md with a reality check assessment.
